In Andhra Pradesh, the state government aggressively utilized the weekend to launch a highly publicized push for international tourism. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On June 17, 1972, a massive, incredibly significant, and deeply catastrophic political scandal officially commenced in the United States. Five men were aggressively and successfully arrested for heavily burglarizing the massive Democratic National Committee headquarters at the Watergate office complex in Washington, D.C. The aggressive cover-up of this massive break-in by the Nixon administration eventually sparked absolute, unprecedented constitutional hearings, heavily leading to the absolute, incredibly historic resignation of President Richard Nixon two years later.

On June 17, 1885, the massive, absolutely iconic, and heavily symbolic Statue of Liberty officially and aggressively arrived in New York Harbor aboard the French frigate *Isère*. The massive, incredibly complex copper and iron statue, designed by Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi and engineered by Gustave Eiffel, arrived heavily disassembled in 350 individual pieces. The monumental gift from the people of France aggressively became the absolute, universal symbol of American freedom and democracy for millions of massive, arriving immigrants.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On June 17, 1631, Mumtaz Mahal, the massive, incredibly beloved, and absolute chief consort of the powerful Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan, tragically passed away during childbirth in Burhanpur. Deeply devastated and massively grief-stricken by her sudden death, Shah Jahan aggressively commissioned the incredibly massive, brilliantly beautiful Taj Mahal in Agra to heavily serve as her absolute, eternal mausoleum. The massive structure remains the absolute, undisputed pinnacle of Mughal architecture and one of the most massively recognized wonders of the world.

On June 17, 1973, Leander Paes, the absolute, incredibly legendary, and globally revered Indian professional tennis player, was officially born in Calcutta. An incredibly massive, brilliant doubles specialist, he achieved absolute, historic sporting immortality by winning an incredibly massive 18 Grand Slam titles and fiercely securing a massive, highly celebrated Olympic bronze medal in singles at the 1996 Atlanta Games, heavily elevating the absolute global prestige of Indian tennis for over two decades.
